sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: 1. FC Heidenheim has shown improvement with an unbeaten run of 3 matches, including 2 clean sheets and a strong 3-0 away win. SV Werder Bremen, while unbeaten in their last 7 matches, has drawn 3 of their last 5 games, indicating inconsistency in securing wins.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last 2 encounters, matches have been high-scoring, with both teams scoring in 5 of their last 6 meetings. The most recent game ended in a thrilling 3-3 draw, and Heidenheim won the earlier matchup 2-1 away.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Heidenheim struggles offensively, ranking 14th in goals scored (36), and defensively, conceding 60 goals (15th). Werder Bremen, positioned 8th, has a balanced attack (50 goals) but a leaky defense (56 conceded), suggesting potential for goals on both sides.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Marvin Pieringer (Heidenheim) and Marvin Ducksch (Werder Bremen) are pivotal, with Pieringer contributing 7 goals and Ducksch adding 7 goals and 7 assists. Ducksch’s standout performance in head-to-heads (4 goals) could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics
sections.text: Heidenheim’s defensive solidity in recent games contrasts with Werder’s tendency to concede, while both teams’ head-to-head history points to an open game. Expect a competitive match with chances for both sides.
